note: Fresh 10-story RedNote import on 2026-06-17 using unique local collector records after 2026-05-12. Source records did not include comments.

stories.title: DC/Virginia marriage green card biometrics and field office confusion
stories.body: A DC/Virginia marriage-based green card applicant married to a U.S. citizen shared an early adjustment timeline. The package was mailed to the Illinois lockbox on April 24, 2026, receipt arrived April 27, biometrics were scheduled May 8, and biometrics were completed May 12. The applicant was confused because the USCIS API suggested the case was at the Washington field office, while several Emma chats said it was still at NBC.

stories.title: Bay Area J1 to marriage green card early filing timeline
stories.body: A Bay Area applicant adjusting from J1 through marriage to a U.S. citizen shared an early filing timeline. The package showed delivered on April 29, 2026. A receipt number arrived on May 4, paper receipts arrived on May 9, and biometrics were completed in San Francisco on May 28.

stories.title: Chicago lockbox marriage green card timeline with interview scheduled
stories.body: An F1 beneficiary married to a U.S. citizen shared a detailed concurrent filing timeline through the Chicago lockbox. They prepared forms and supporting documents through April, filed I-130 online and mailed I-485, I-765, I-864, and related forms on May 6, and USCIS received the package on May 8. Payments were charged May 13. Biometrics notice and online access arrived in late May, biometrics were moved up and completed May 27, and by May 31 both the online I-130 and mailed I-485 showed an interview scheduled for June 18.

stories.title: I-751 removal of conditions filed before conditional card expiration
stories.body: A conditional green card holder shared an I-751 removal-of-conditions timeline. The conditional card was expiring August 3, and the applicant filed online on May 8, about 88 days before expiration. The case number arrived May 11, the 48-month extension notice arrived May 13, the biometrics notice arrived May 29, and biometrics were scheduled for June 1.

stories.title: San Jose marriage green card case waiting after interview
stories.body: An F1 applicant married to a U.S. citizen shared a San Jose marriage green card timeline. The I-485 package was sent February 27, 2026, delivered March 3, and payment plus paper notices followed March 5. Biometrics were scheduled for April 3 but moved up to March 23. The interview was scheduled April 8 for May 13, and after the interview the case moved to active review. The applicant was anxious because the EAD was expiring soon and the I-485 had not yet updated.

stories.title: Long Island marriage green card interview with follow-up uploads
stories.body: An F1 beneficiary married to a U.S. citizen filed I-130, I-485, and I-765 without a lawyer. I-130 was filed online March 15, I-485 and I-765 were mailed March 20, notices and payment confirmations arrived in late March, biometrics were completed April 6, and the interview was invited April 7. At the May 14 Long Island field office interview, the officer asked about travel history, prior addresses, relationship history, photos, and I-485 yes/no questions. The officer said the relationship looked fine but asked for more address evidence, which the couple uploaded that night. I-130 was later approved, while I-485 remained pending and the applicant kept checking for updates.

stories.title: Spouse immigrant visa timeline from I-130 to interview letter
stories.body: A consular spouse green card applicant shared a timeline from I-130 filing to interview scheduling. I-130 was filed July 5, 2024, K3 was filed August 19, an initial evidence request came September 17, 2025, and the I-130 approval email arrived September 26. The case was sent to the Department of State on October 2, the NVC email arrived October 3, documents were accepted November 21, documentary qualification arrived February 19, 2026, and the interview letter arrived May 19 for a July 21 interview.

stories.title: Marriage AOS approval after interview despite living separately
stories.body: A marriage adjustment applicant shared that the package reached the lockbox January 15, receipts appeared February 3, biometrics were scheduled February 7 and completed February 9, and an interview notice arrived April 9. The interview happened May 19, I-130 was approved the next day, and I-485 was approved May 22. The couple had a nonstandard fact pattern because they were not living together for practical reasons, but they disclosed that honestly and brought strong evidence such as joint accounts and family photos.

stories.title: Los Angeles F2A marriage green card approved after long wait
stories.body: A Los Angeles F2A marriage green card applicant, married to a permanent resident, shared a long timeline. The package was sent and received in August 2023, receipts and access code arrived in September, standalone I-765 was approved in late September and the EAD arrived in October, I-130 was approved in November 2023, and I-131 was approved in June 2024. The I-485 interview happened May 6, 2026, the I-485 was approved May 7, and the green card arrived May 15.

stories.title: Spouse I-485 approval after interview delays and silent updates
stories.body: A spouse green card applicant shared that USCIS received the case August 6, 2024. Biometrics were scheduled August 16, medical exam work was scheduled August 28, and an interview notice came September 22, 2025 for an October 27 interview. The applicant forgot some documents at the interview and uploaded them later, then waited through several silent updates. I-485 approval arrived May 13, 2026, and the card arrived May 21.
